title: Biotechnology and genetics
explanation:
Biotechnology and genetics

  • genome, mutation, hereditary, embryo, sequencing
  • CRISPR, ethics, therapy, clone, modification
  • verbs: engineer, inherit, diagnose, manipulate

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Scientists mapped the entire human ____.  | *genome | genre | gym
The rare ____ increases cancer risk. | *mutation | mansion | motion
Some conditions are ____ and pass through families. | *hereditary | heroic | harmful only
Gene ____ raises profound ethical questions. | *editing | eating | ending

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: The lab uses ____ to alter specific DNA sequences. | CRISPR
Doctors hope gene ____ can treat inherited diseases. | therapy
Researchers must not ____ data to support a theory. | manipulate
Tests can ____ disorders before symptoms appear. | diagnose
Children ____ traits from both parents. | inherit

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: DNA ____ has become faster and cheaper. | *sequencing | seasoning | seating
Research on human ____ is tightly regulated. | *embryos | embargo | emblems
Critics fear unsafe genetic ____.  | *modification | motivation | migration
Scientists can ____ cells to resist certain viruses. | *engineer | enjoy | enroll
